# @fluenti/vite-plugin

> Vite plugin for Fluenti — build-time i18n template transforms, virtual modules, and per-route code splitting.

Docs: https://fluenti.dev
GitHub: https://github.com/usefluenti/fluenti/tree/main/packages/vite-plugin
License: MIT

## Install

```bash
pnpm add -D @fluenti/vite-plugin
```

Peer dependency: `vite ^5 || ^6 || ^8`
Runtime dependency: `@fluenti/core`

## Setup

```ts
// vite.config.ts
import vue from '@vitejs/plugin-vue'
import fluenti from '@fluenti/vite-plugin'

export default {
  plugins: [
    vue(),
    fluenti({
      framework: 'vue',      // 'vue' | 'react' | 'solid' | 'auto'
      splitting: 'dynamic',  // 'dynamic' | 'static' | 'per-route' | false
    }),
  ],
}
```

## Options

```ts
interface FluentiPluginOptions {
  /** Framework mode (default: 'auto') */
  framework?: 'vue' | 'react' | 'solid' | 'auto'
  /** Directory containing compiled message catalogs (default: 'src/locales/compiled') */
  catalogDir?: string
  /** Source locale (default: 'en') */
  sourceLocale?: string
  /** Available locales (default: [sourceLocale]) */
  locales?: string[]
  /** Code splitting strategy (default: 'dynamic') */
  splitting?: 'dynamic' | 'static' | 'per-route' | false
  /** Default locale for build-time static strategy (default: sourceLocale) */
  defaultBuildLocale?: string
}
```

## Plugin Architecture

The default export `fluentiPlugin(options?)` returns an array of six Vite plugins that run in sequence:

1. **`fluenti:virtual`** — resolves and loads virtual modules (`virtual:fluenti/messages/<locale>`, plus split-mode virtual IDs). Handles `resolveId` and `load` hooks.
2. **`fluenti:vue-template`** (`enforce: 'pre'`) — SFC pre-transform for Vue `.vue` files. Rewrites `v-t` directives, `<Trans>`, and `<Plural>` in `<template>` blocks before the Vue compiler runs.
3. **`fluenti:solid-jsx`** (`enforce: 'pre'`) — compile-time JSX transform for Solid `.tsx`/`.jsx` files. Rewrites `<Trans>` and `<Plural>` components.
4. **`fluenti:script-transform`** (`enforce: 'pre'`) — transforms `t` tagged template literals and `t()` function calls in script blocks across all frameworks. Auto-injects `useI18n` imports.
5. **`fluenti:build-split`** (no enforce, runs after Vue compilation) — build-time transform that replaces `$t()` calls with direct catalog lookups for code splitting. In `per-route` mode, emits per-route and shared message chunk assets during `generateBundle`.
6. **`fluenti:dev`** — development server integration. Triggers HMR for virtual `fluenti` modules when catalog files change.

## Compile-time Transforms

### v-t Directive (Vue Templates)

A compile-time AST transform (not a runtime directive). Runs during Vue template compilation.

Supported patterns:

| Template | Output |
|----------|--------|
| `<tag v-t>text</tag>` | `<tag>{{ $t('text') }}</tag>` |
| `<tag v-t:explicit.id>text</tag>` | `<tag>{{ $t('explicit.id') }}</tag>` |
| `<tag v-t.plural="countExpr">one \| other</tag>` | `<tag>{{ $t('{countExpr, plural, one {one} other {other}}', { countExpr }) }}</tag>` |
| `<tag v-t.alt alt="text" />` | `<tag :alt="$t('text')" />` |
| `<tag v-t.placeholder placeholder="text" />` | `<tag :placeholder="$t('text')" />` |

Attribute modifiers: `alt`, `placeholder`, `title`, `aria-label`, `label`.

Rich text with child elements uses `$vtRich()`:
```html
<p v-t>Click <a href="/help">here</a> for help</p>
<!-- becomes -->
<p v-html="$vtRich('Click <0>here</0> for help', [{tag: 'a', attrs: {href: '/help'}}])"></p>
```

Template interpolations are extracted as ICU placeholders:
```html
<p v-t>Hello {{ user.name }}</p>
<!-- becomes -->
<p>{{ $t('Hello {name}', { name: user.name }) }}</p>
```

### <Trans> Component (Vue Templates)

Compiles away the `<Trans>` component at build time:

```html
<Trans>Hello world</Trans>
<!-- becomes -->
<span>{{ $t('Hello world') }}</span>

<Trans tag="p">Click <a href="/help">here</a></Trans>
<!-- becomes -->
<p v-html="$vtRich('Click <0>here</0>', [{tag: 'a', attrs: {href: '/help'}}])"></p>
```

The `tag` prop controls the wrapper element (default: `'span'`). If a `message` prop is present, the transform is skipped (old API, handled at runtime).

### <Plural> Component (Vue Templates)

Two forms supported:

**String props** (simple plurals):
```html
<Plural :value="count" one="one item" other="{count} items" />
<!-- becomes -->
<span v-text="$t('{count, plural, one {one item} other {{count} items}}', { count })"></span>
```

**Named slots** (rich text plurals):
```html
<Plural :value="count">
  <template #one>one <b>item</b></template>
  <template #other>{{ count }} <b>items</b></template>
</Plural>
<!-- becomes -->
<span v-html="$vtRich('{count, plural, one {one <0>item</0>} other {{count} <1>items</1>}}', [...elements], { count })"></span>
```

Supported CLDR plural categories: `zero`, `one`, `two`, `few`, `many`, `other`. The `zero` category maps to ICU `=0` (exact match).

### t`` Tagged Template Literals (All Frameworks)

Transforms tagged template literals in `<script>` blocks:

```ts
const greeting = t`Hello ${user.name}`
```

Framework-specific output:
- **Vue**: `computed(() => __i18n.t('Hello {name}', { name: unref(user.name) }))`
- **React**: `__i18n.t('Hello {name}', { name: user.name })`
- **Solid**: `createMemo(() => __i18n.t('Hello {name}', { name: user.name }))`

### t() Function Calls (All Frameworks)

Standalone `t()` calls (not `.t()` or `$t()`) are rewritten:

```ts
t('Hello world')        // → __i18n.t('Hello world')
t('Hello {name}', obj)  // → __i18n.t('Hello {name}', obj)
```

Auto-injected imports per framework:
- **Vue**: `import { useI18n as __useI18n } from '@fluenti/vue'` + `computed`/`unref` from `'vue'`
- **React**: `import { __useI18n } from '@fluenti/react'`
- **Solid**: `import { useI18n as __useI18n } from '@fluenti/solid'` + lazy proxy to defer context resolution

### Solid JSX Transform

For `.tsx`/`.jsx` files when framework is `'solid'` or auto-detected as Solid:

```tsx
<Trans>Hello world</Trans>
// → <span>{__i18n.t('Hello world')}</span>

<Plural value={count()} one="one item" other="{count} items" />
// → <span textContent={__i18n.t('{count(), plural, one {one item} other {{count} items}}', {count()})} />
```

Rich text `<Trans>` with child JSX elements is not transformed at compile time — the runtime `<Trans>` component handles it.

## Code Splitting Strategies

### `'dynamic'` (default)

Lazy-load locale catalogs on demand. The default locale is loaded statically; other locales are loaded when `switchLocale()` is called. At build time, `$t('hash')` calls are replaced with direct catalog property lookups (`__catalog._hash`), and a catalog import from `virtual:fluenti/runtime` is injected.

### `false`

No splitting. All messages bundled together in a single chunk.

### `'static'`

All messages from `defaultBuildLocale` are inlined at build time. Ideal for SSR where only one locale is needed per request. Uses `virtual:fluenti/messages` for static re-exports.

### `'per-route'`

Messages are automatically split by route during `generateBundle`. Each route chunk gets only the messages it uses. Shared messages (used by 2+ chunks) go into a common chunk.

Output assets:
- `_fluenti/shared-{locale}.js` — messages shared across routes
- `_fluenti/{routeName}-{locale}.js` — route-specific messages

Uses `virtual:fluenti/route-runtime` with `__loadRoute()` for loading route chunks.

The plugin tracks which message hashes are used in each module via the `buildSplitPlugin` transform phase, then classifies them as shared vs. route-specific during `generateBundle`.

## Virtual Modules

### `virtual:fluenti/messages/{locale}`

Re-exports the compiled catalog for a locale:
```ts
export { default } from 'src/locales/compiled/{locale}.js'
```

### `virtual:fluenti/runtime`

Reactive catalog for dynamic splitting. Provides `__switchLocale()` and `__preloadLocale()`.

### `virtual:fluenti/messages`

Static re-export for SSR builds (static splitting).

### `virtual:fluenti/route-runtime`

Per-route catalog runtime. Provides `__loadRoute()` for loading route-specific message chunks.

## Framework Auto-detection

When `framework` is `'auto'` (default), the plugin detects the framework per file:

1. `.vue` file extension → `'vue'`
2. Code contains `solid-js`, `createSignal`, or `createMemo` → `'solid'`
3. Code contains `react`, `useState`, `useEffect`, or `jsx` → `'react'`
4. Fallback → `'vue'`

## HMR Support

The `fluenti:dev` plugin watches for changes in the `catalogDir`. When a compiled catalog file changes, it invalidates all `virtual:fluenti` modules in the module graph, triggering hot module replacement.

## Exports

### Default Export

```ts
export default function fluentiPlugin(options?: FluentiPluginOptions): Plugin[]
```

Main plugin factory. Returns an array of six Vite plugins.

### Named Exports

```ts
export function createVtNodeTransform(): NodeTransform
```

Vue compiler AST `nodeTransform` for the `v-t` directive. Can be configured manually via `@vitejs/plugin-vue`'s `compilerOptions.nodeTransforms` if the automatic SFC pre-transform is insufficient.

```ts
export function transformSolidJsx(code: string): { code: string; changed: boolean }
```

Compile-time JSX transform for Solid `<Trans>` and `<Plural>` components. Exported for direct use or testing.

```ts
export type { FluentiPluginOptions } from './types'
export type NodeTransform = (node: ASTNode, context: TransformContext) => void | (() => void)
```

### Client Types

The package exports `./client` with type declarations for virtual modules:
```ts
import messages from 'virtual:fluenti/messages/en'
```
